fr.recommendations: Pruning and maintaining dense vegetation near pedestrian walkways could facilitate better visibility and easier movement for emergency responders. Enhancing side access routes by strategically trimming or rearranging landscape elements may assist in improving the maneuverability of emergency vehicles and personnel. Clearly marked pedestrian pathways and designated access points around the main building could support effective responder navigation and operational efficiency. Ensuring these access points remain unobstructed and clearly identifiable would support the quick and effective staging of emergency activities.

wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space by increasing separation between vegetation and structures could significantly improve property preparedness. Thinning the dense tree canopy and removing lower branches may reduce vertical fuel continuity and contribute to a less favorable environment for fire spread. Mitigating the vegetated perimeter by creating breaks or thinning concentrated areas could help to limit the continuous fuel path, which may otherwise facilitate fire encroachment. Scattered trees would benefit from additional spacing to increase interruptions in fuel continuity. Addressing the vegetation between structures by selectively thinning or managing overly dense areas may improve exposure reduction. Implementing these vegetation management strategies can support both immediate defensible space and long-term resilience to wildfire impacts while improving access and safety for potential suppression operations.
